publication.title: Rigging for Trail Work: Principles, Techniques, and Lessons from the Backcountry
publication.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, National Technology and Development Program
publication.extent: viii, 176 manual pages; 188 digital object pages

license.name: eligible United States Government text treated as presumptively public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105
license.scope: manually transformed eligible federal text only
license.limitations: No blanket rights conclusion is made for embedded or credited third-party components. Photographs, figures, expressive source tables, decorative quotations, vendor prose, and externally supplied matter are excluded.

entries.reason: Canonical source for manual review; large binary omitted after identity and integrity were recorded.

word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately
category_scope_note: The uplines_suspension_hardpoints mapping covers mechanics and uncertainty reasoning only; it provides no human-suspension, tree, or indoor-hardpoint certification.

domain_transfer.allowed: mechanics, inspection, planning, communication, and failure-consequence reasoning with source context attached
domain_transfer.not_allowed: human-support design values; bondage-rope approval; tree, limb, indoor hardpoint, frame, tripod, anchor, or hardware certification; universalized Forest Service, manufacturer, or OSHA rules
domain_transfer.controlling_authority: current manufacturer instructions, applicable domain standards, and qualified structural or rigging evaluation

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: mechanics, assumptions, limitations, inspection logic, and source-attributed contextual comparisons
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: URL recall, title or author recall, page-number recall, identifier trivia, vendor recall, or answers detached from scope and assumptions

sampling_guidance.reason: This roughly 6,100-word manual digest should not dominate domain sampling simply because it is longer than individual bondage-source documents.
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: source-document-aware chunks preserving section warning and printed-page citations

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign this entire canonical source document to one split before Markdown chunking or QA derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude validation, OOD, shadow, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
